teaching_card.title: Right Triangles (Easy)
teaching_card.body: In a right triangle, the Pythagorean theorem links the legs a and b to the hypotenuse c: a² + b² = c². Recognizing common triples like 3-4-5 saves time.
key_points: a² + b² = c² (c is the hypotenuse).
The hypotenuse is the longest side.

screens.title: The Pythagorean theorem
screens.body: In a right triangle, the legs a and b and the hypotenuse c (the side opposite the right angle) satisfy a² + b² = c². The hypotenuse is always the longest side.
key_points: a² + b² = c² (c is the hypotenuse).
The hypotenuse is opposite the right angle and is longest.

screens.title: Finding a side
screens.body: To find the hypotenuse, add the squares of the legs and take the square root. To find a leg, subtract: leg = √(c² − a²). Don't just add or subtract the side lengths themselves.

screens.title: Special right triangles
screens.body: Two come up a lot. 45-45-90: the legs are equal and the hypotenuse is leg·√2. 30-60-90: sides are in the ratio 1 : √3 : 2 (short leg, longer leg, hypotenuse). Memorizing these saves time.

screens.title: Try the moves
screens.body: Legs 3 and 4: hypotenuse √(9+16) = √25 = 5. A 45-45-90 triangle with legs 5 has hypotenuse 5√2. A 30-60-90 with short leg 6 has longer leg 6√3 and hypotenuse 12.
